summary: Implement file watcher using fsnotify that monitors a directory for JSONL file changes, detects appends, and emits WatchEvent with FilePath, Offset, and Lines. Includes offset tracking, JSONL-only filtering, clean shutdown, and idempotent Start/Stop.
keyDecisions: 2.6: Used fsnotify for OS-native file change events, no polling fallback needed as fsnotify handles cross-platform
2.6: Offset tracking via map[string]int64 to remember last-known file sizes per path
2.6: Only .jsonl files are processed; all other file events are filtered out in handleEvent
2.6: Buffered channel (cap 16) for events to reduce contention between producer and consumer
2.6: New files discovered via Create event emit all existing lines; existing files only emit new appends

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: internal/watcher/watcher.go implements the Watcher interface with Start(), Stop(), Events() <-chan WatchEvent
WatchEvent carries FilePath, Offset, and raw new Lines
Stop() cleanly closes the watcher and channel
Integration tests using t.TempDir() for isolated fixture files
3-second timeout on test waits to prevent hangs

notes: fsnotify provides cross-platform support including Windows (ReadDirectoryChangesW). No separate polling fallback needed.
